notes:
XY-era set (xy5, Primal Clash, released 2015-02-06). STRUCTURE: 10 cards/pack + 1 basic Energy (not modeled). Confirmed by Pokemon Center XY sleeved-booster listing ('10 Cards') and the era baseline: 4 common, 3 uncommon, 1 reverse-holo, 1 rare-or-better. Each XY pack is guaranteed 1 rare-or-better and 1 holo foil. Booster box = 36 packs; ETB = 8 packs. A sealed 36-pack box AND an Elite Trainer Box both existed for Primal Clash -> shipEv=true; display.packs=36, etb.packs=8.

AVAILABLE-BUCKET CHECK (re-derived, not taken on faith): project snapshot data/snapshot/snapshot.json -> sets['primal-clash'] buckets its 164 cards EXACTLY to common 39, uncommon 50, rare 25, rare-holo 18, ultra-rare 28, secret-rare 4 -- identical to the task's stated available buckets. CRUCIAL: this set has NO separate 'ex' bucket -- all 28 Pokemon-EX / Mega-EX / Full-Art cards (Primal Kyogre EX, Primal Groudon EX, Gardevoir EX, the M-EX's, the Full Arts) bucket as 'ultra-rare', so the value hits resolve across {rare, rare-holo, ultra-rare, secret-rare} only. All distribution keys used are a strict subset; no missing bucket added.

RARE-OR-BETTER SLOT (the single guaranteed rare-or-better card) -- mapped by SUMMING fine XY rates onto the 4 buckets:
- ultra-rare = 0.1667 (~1:6 packs, ~6 ultras per 36-pack box). Sourced two ways. (a) Flipside Gaming 'Comprehensive Review of Rarity Part Two': XY EX/UR pull rates became 'more favorable to collectors' as sets loaded up on EX/UR cards; 'By Ancient Origins, pull rates had dropped to approximately 1:6 packs for EXs and 1:12 packs for URs'. Primal Clash is a mid-XY, EX-heavy set (28 EX-or-better cards) sitting just before that generous extreme, so combined EX+FA+M-EX ~1:6 packs. (b) Elite Fourum Primal Clash thread: community consensus '4-6 ultras typically Normal for an English booster box', with EX-heavy Primal Clash expected to keep generous rates ('with over 30 EX or better cards they had to do something about the pull rates'); prerelease samples skewed to 7-8/box, so a retail-realistic ~6/box (0.1667) is taken, not the inflated prerelease high.
- secret-rare = 0.0139 (~1:72 packs, ~0.5 per box, ~1 per 2 boxes; the 4 secrets combined: Primal Kyogre EX 152, Primal Groudon EX 153, Wailord EX, Switch 163). Flipside Part Two verbatim: 'Secret Rares continued to retain their 1:72 pack pull rate.'
- rare-holo = 0.40 (~14.4 holo rares per box). Cross-check: Elite Fourum opener blastoiserules1 reported ~9 holos + 2 plain rares among the non-ultra slots in 23 retail packs (~0.39 holo share), matching the ~0.40 holo-rate convention for the XY rare slot.
- rare = remainder = 1 - 0.1667 - 0.0139 - 0.40 = 0.4194 (plain non-holo rare).
Slot sums to EXACTLY 1.0 (verified: 0.4194+0.40+0.1667+0.0139=1.0). Per-box economy: ~6 ultra, ~14.4 holo, ~0.5 secret, ~15.1 plain rare -- internally consistent with both Flipside era figures and Elite Fourum community samples.

REVERSE-HOLO SLOT: per dataset convention, reverse-holo filler modeled with the plain common/uncommon ids (market value is what matters). Neutral approximation 50/50 common/uncommon. Sums to 1.0.

CONFIDENCE = low (honest): no large PUBLISHED per-set Primal Clash pull-rate STUDY exists. The EX/UR ~1:6, secret 1:72 and holo ~0.40 figures are XY-era community/retailer averages (Flipside Gaming aggregation + Elite Fourum community openings) mapped onto Primal Clash, not a Primal-Clash-specific large sample. Vintage sealed XY boxes carry a collector premium, so EV/booster will sit far below sealed box price -- expected; rates were NOT inflated to compensate (prerelease 7-8/box highs were deliberately pulled down to a retail-realistic 6/box).

INTEGRITY: every slot sums to exactly 1.0; all distribution keys {common, uncommon, rare, rare-holo, ultra-rare, secret-rare} are a strict subset of this set's available buckets; no invented per-set percentages -- the 1:6 EX-or-better, 1:72 secret, and ~0.40 holo are the sourced era figures.
